understanding.function: Nuclear fuel reprocessing system for extracting fissile material (uranium and plutonium) from spent nuclear fuel waste, capable of processing multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with 1800 tHM/year capacity
understanding.domain: Nuclear fuel cycle - spent fuel reprocessing and fissile material recovery
constraints: Product purity standards for downstream fuel fabrication
uncertainties: Specific technical processes and equipment details not defined
Exact safety and regulatory requirements beyond ALARA principle
Interface specifications with upstream waste reception and downstream fuel fabrication systems
Detailed operational parameters and control systems
Emergency response and accident scenarios not described

candidates.name: PUREX Process
candidates.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene for solvent extraction of uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
candidates.function_alignment: Primary industrial method for fissile material extraction from spent fuel, proven at 1800+ tHM/year scale
candidates.notes: Most widely used commercial reprocessing technology, implemented at La Hague and Sellafield

candidates.name: COEX Process
candidates.description: Co-extraction process that keeps uranium and plutonium together throughout the separation process to reduce proliferation risks
candidates.function_alignment: Alternative to PUREX with enhanced proliferation resistance, suitable for multi-fuel type processing
candidates.notes: Developed for improved security while maintaining separation efficiency

candidates.name: GANEX Process
candidates.description: Group ActiNide EXtraction process for simultaneous recovery of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides using advanced extractants
candidates.function_alignment: Next-generation reprocessing for enhanced waste minimization and actinide recovery
candidates.notes: Under development for future fuel cycles, addresses waste reduction requirements

candidates.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
candidates.description: Shielded containment cells with remote manipulation equipment for handling highly radioactive spent fuel and process materials
candidates.function_alignment: Essential infrastructure for ALARA compliance and safe handling of radioactive materials
candidates.notes: Critical for maintaining radiological protection during reprocessing operations

candidates.name: Pulsed Column Extractors
candidates.description: Solvent extraction equipment using pulsed flow to enhance mass transfer between aqueous and organic phases
candidates.function_alignment: Primary equipment for uranium and plutonium separation in industrial reprocessing
candidates.notes: Standard equipment in PUREX facilities for high-throughput separation

candidates.name: Centrifugal Contactors
candidates.description: High-efficiency solvent extraction equipment using centrifugal force for rapid phase separation and mixing
candidates.function_alignment: Alternative to pulsed columns offering compact design and improved process control
candidates.notes: Suitable for high-capacity operations with reduced footprint

candidates.name: Vitrification Systems
candidates.description: High-level waste immobilization technology converting liquid radioactive waste into stable glass matrix
candidates.function_alignment: Essential for managing high-level waste streams from reprocessing operations
candidates.notes: Required for long-term disposal of non-recoverable fission products

candidates.name: Fuel Dissolution Systems
candidates.description: Nitric acid dissolution systems for converting spent fuel assemblies into aqueous solution for chemical processing
candidates.function_alignment: First step in reprocessing to prepare spent fuel for solvent extraction
candidates.notes: Must handle multiple fuel types (UOX, MOX, ERU/REU) with appropriate dissolution parameters

candidates.name: Off-gas Treatment Systems
candidates.description: Comprehensive gas treatment systems for capturing and treating radioactive gases released during fuel dissolution and processing
candidates.function_alignment: Critical for radiological containment and environmental protection
candidates.notes: Includes iodine, noble gas, and tritium capture systems

candidates.name: Product Purification Systems
candidates.description: Multi-cycle solvent extraction and ion exchange systems for achieving nuclear-grade purity of recovered uranium and plutonium
candidates.function_alignment: Ensures recovered materials meet specifications for fuel fabrication
candidates.notes: Critical for downstream fuel manufacturing quality requirements

candidates.name: Criticality Safety Systems
candidates.description: Engineered systems and administrative controls to prevent inadvertent criticality during processing of fissile materials
candidates.function_alignment: Mandatory safety system for handling concentrated uranium and plutonium solutions
candidates.notes: Includes geometry control, mass limits, and neutron absorbers

candidates.name: Process Control and Instrumentation Systems
candidates.description: Advanced control systems for monitoring and controlling chemical processes, radiation levels, and material flows
candidates.function_alignment: Essential for maintaining process parameters and safety systems
candidates.notes: Includes radiation monitoring, chemical analysis, and automated process control

ranked.name: PUREX Process
ranked.description: Plutonium Uranium Redox EXtraction process using tributyl phosphate (TBP) in kerosene for solvent extraction of uranium and plutonium from spent nuclear fuel
ranked.notes: Proven commercial technology operating at required scale (La Hague 1700 tHM/year). Handles multiple fuel types and meets all functional requirements.

ranked.name: Hot Cell Remote Handling Systems
ranked.description: Shielded containment cells with remote manipulation equipment for handling highly radioactive spent fuel and process materials
ranked.notes: Essential infrastructure for ALARA compliance. Mature technology deployed in all commercial reprocessing facilities.

ranked.name: Fuel Dissolution Systems
ranked.description: Nitric acid dissolution systems for converting spent fuel assemblies into aqueous solution for chemical processing
ranked.notes: Proven head-end technology capable of handling UOX, MOX, and ERU/REU fuel types. Critical first step in reprocessing chain.

ranked.name: Pulsed Column Extractors
ranked.description: Solvent extraction equipment using pulsed flow to enhance mass transfer between aqueous and organic phases
ranked.notes: Standard equipment in PUREX facilities. Proven at industrial scale for high-throughput uranium and plutonium separation.

ranked.name: Product Purification Systems
ranked.description: Multi-cycle solvent extraction and ion exchange systems for achieving nuclear-grade purity of recovered uranium and plutonium
ranked.notes: Essential for meeting fuel fabrication specifications. Well-established technology in commercial reprocessing.

ranked.name: Off-gas Treatment Systems
ranked.description: Comprehensive gas treatment systems for capturing and treating radioactive gases released during fuel dissolution and processing
ranked.notes: Critical for radiological containment and regulatory compliance. Mature technology with proven performance.

ranked.name: Criticality Safety Systems
ranked.description: Engineered systems and administrative controls to prevent inadvertent criticality during processing of fissile materials
ranked.notes: Mandatory safety system with well-established design principles. Essential for fissile material handling operations.

ranked.name: Process Control and Instrumentation Systems
ranked.description: Advanced control systems for monitoring and controlling chemical processes, radiation levels, and material flows
ranked.notes: Essential for operational control and safety. Modern systems available but require integration with reprocessing-specific requirements.

ranked.name: Centrifugal Contactors
ranked.description: High-efficiency solvent extraction equipment using centrifugal force for rapid phase separation and mixing
ranked.notes: Alternative to pulsed columns with advantages but less industrial deployment at 1800 tHM/year scale.

ranked.name: Vitrification Systems
ranked.description: High-level waste immobilization technology converting liquid radioactive waste into stable glass matrix
ranked.notes: Proven technology for waste management but not directly involved in fissile material extraction function.

ranked.name: COEX Process
ranked.description: Co-extraction process that keeps uranium and plutonium together throughout the separation process to reduce proliferation risks
ranked.notes: Under development with limited industrial demonstration. Enhanced proliferation resistance but unproven at required scale.

ranked.name: GANEX Process
ranked.description: Group ActiNide EXtraction process for simultaneous recovery of uranium, plutonium, and minor actinides using advanced extractants
ranked.notes: Research-stage technology for future fuel cycles. Not ready for industrial deployment at 1800 tHM/year capacity.
